title: GitHub Review by greptile-apps[bot]
summary: The reviewer identifies that `normalizePodSpecDefaults` in `job.go` only normalizes container-level defaults but misses pod-level API-server defaults (`ServiceAccountName`, `DNSPolicy`, `SchedulerName`, `TerminationGracePeriodSeconds`, `EnableServiceLinks`), causing spec comparisons to always detect a diff and trigger unnecessary updates/recreations on every reconcile. The same pod-level defaulting gap affects `EnsureDeployment` and `EnsureCronJob`, producing infinite update loops and preventing stable readiness signals in production. The fix is to extend `normalizePodSpecDefaults` with the missing pod-level defaults and apply it to the desired spec before comparison in all three code paths (Job, CronJob, Deployment).

code_comments.author: greptile-apps[bot]
code_comments.resolution: [0] job.go:normalizePodSpecDefaults was extended with the five pod-level defaults (ServiceAccountName='default', DNSPolicy=ClusterFirst, SchedulerName='default-scheduler', TerminationGracePeriodSeconds=30, EnableServiceLinks=true). The testJobWithDefaults helper in job_test.go was updated to include these pod-level defaults, the existing TestRunJob_existingComplete_apiServerDefaults test was updated to use the expanded helper and assert on ServiceAccountName, verifying the fix prevents spurious delete+recreate.

code_comments.body:
**API-server pod-level defaults cause an infinite spec-update loop**

`apiequality.Semantic.DeepEqual(existing.Spec, deploy.Spec)` compares the full `DeploymentSpec`, including the pod template's `PodSpec`. When a caller constructs a `Deployment` without setting `ServiceAccountName`, `DNSPolicy`, `SchedulerName`, `TerminationGracePeriodSeconds`, or `EnableServiceLinks`, the API server writes the defaults into `existing.Spec.Template.Spec`, while `deploy.Spec.Template.Spec` retains the zero values. Because `apiequality.Semantic` uses `reflect.DeepEqual` for plain string and integer fields, `""` ≠ `"default"` and `nil` ≠ `&30`, so the guard is **always false** in production.

Consequences:
1. `c.Update` is called on every reconcile even when nothing actually changed.
2. The API server increments `Generation` on each update.
3. `IsDeploymentReady` returns `false` while `ObservedGeneration` lags, so the reconciler never sees a stable `ready=true` signal.
4. This is an infinite update loop: the deployment controller reacts to the spec-update, the operator reconciles again, detects the (spurious) diff, and updates again.

Unit tests miss this because the fake client does not apply API-server pod-spec defaulting.

The most robust fix is to normalize the pod-level defaults on `deploy.Spec.Template.Spec` before the comparison (analogous to `normalizePodSpecDefaults` in `job.go`), or to apply `controllerutil.CreateOrUpdate` which uses a server-side PATCH and avoids the object-level diff entirely.

`````markdown
Line: 43-52

Because `apiequality.Semantic` uses `reflect.DeepEqual` for plain string and integer fields, `""` ≠ `"default"` and `nil` ≠ `&30`, so the guard is **always false** in production.

Consequences:
1. `c.Update` is called on every reconcile even when nothing actually changed.
2. The API server increments `Generation` on each update.
3. `IsDeploymentReady` returns `false` while `ObservedGeneration` lags, so the reconciler never sees a stable `ready=true` signal.
4. This is an infinite update loop: the deployment controller reacts to the spec-update, the operator reconciles again, detects the (spurious) diff, and updates again.

Unit tests miss this because the fake client does not apply API-server pod-spec defaulting.

The most robust fix is to normalize the pod-level defaults on `deploy.Spec.Template.Spec` before the comparison (analogous to `normalizePodSpecDefaults` in `job.go`), or to apply `controllerutil.CreateOrUpdate` which uses a server-side PATCH and avoids the object-level diff entirely.

How can I resolve this? If you propose a fix, please make it concise.
`````
</details>
code_comments.author: greptile-apps[bot]
code_comments.resolution: [1] deployment.go:EnsureDeployment was updated to deep-copy the desired spec and call normalizePodSpecDefaults on it before comparing with the existing spec. Since deployment.go had no normalization functions, complete copies of normalizePodSpecDefaults (with pod-level defaults), normalizeContainerDefaults, and defaultImagePullPolicy were added. A new TestEnsureDeployment_apiServerDefaults test was added that creates an existing Deployment with API-server defaults and verifies a desired Deployment with zero-value fields does not trigger a spurious update.

code_comments.body:
**`EnsureCronJob` spec comparison is sensitive to API-server pod-level defaults**

`apiequality.Semantic.DeepEqual(existing.Spec, cronJob.Spec)` compares the full `CronJobSpec`, which includes `JobTemplate.Spec.Template.Spec`. The same API-server pod-level defaulting issue that affects `EnsureDeployment` applies here: callers that leave `ServiceAccountName`, `DNSPolicy`, `SchedulerName`, `TerminationGracePeriodSeconds`, or `EnableServiceLinks` at their zero values will produce a stored spec that differs from the desired spec on every reconcile.

The consequence is `c.Update` being called on every reconcile — continuous unnecessary churn on the CronJob resource and spurious watch events. Unlike `RunJob`, this does not delete-recreate, but it still causes the CronJob controller to reprocess the CronJob on every operator reconcile cycle.

<details><summary>Prompt To Fix With AI</summary>

`````markdown
This is a comment left during a code review.
Path: internal/common/job/job.go
Line: 103-108

Comment:
**`EnsureCronJob` spec comparison is sensitive to API-server pod-level defaults**

`apiequality.Semantic.DeepEqual(existing.Spec, cronJob.Spec)` compares the full `CronJobSpec`, which includes `JobTemplate.Spec.Template.Spec`. The same API-server pod-level defaulting issue that affects `EnsureDeployment` applies here: callers that leave `ServiceAccountName`, `DNSPolicy`, `SchedulerName`, `TerminationGracePeriodSeconds`, or `EnableServiceLinks` at their zero values will produce a stored spec that differs from the desired spec on every reconcile.

The consequence is `c.Update` being called on every reconcile — continuous unnecessary churn on the CronJob resource and spurious watch events. Unlike `RunJob`, this does not delete-recreate, but it still causes the CronJob controller to reprocess the CronJob on every operator reconcile cycle.

How can I resolve this? If you propose a fix, please make it concise.
`````
</details>
code_comments.author: greptile-apps[bot]
code_comments.resolution: [2] job.go:EnsureCronJob was updated to deep-copy the desired CronJobSpec, normalize its pod-level defaults via normalizePodSpecDefaults on the JobTemplate's PodSpec, and compare the normalized copy against the existing spec. A new TestEnsureCronJob_apiServerDefaults test was added that creates a CronJob with API-server defaults pre-populated and verifies no spurious update occurs when the desired CronJob has zero-value pod fields.

resolution_summary: All three review comments were addressed by adding the five missing pod-level API-server defaults (ServiceAccountName, DNSPolicy, SchedulerName, TerminationGracePeriodSeconds, EnableServiceLinks) to the normalizePodSpecDefaults function in both job.go and deployment.go, and by normalizing the desired spec before comparison in EnsureCronJob and EnsureDeployment. The deployment.go file received a full copy of the normalization functions (normalizePodSpecDefaults, normalizeContainerDefaults, defaultImagePullPolicy) since it previously had no normalization at all, and its EnsureDeployment function was updated to deep-copy and normalize the desired spec before comparing. Corresponding unit tests were added or updated in all three locations — job_test.go's testJobWithDefaults helper was expanded with pod-level defaults and a new TestEnsureCronJob_apiServerDefaults test was added, while deployment_test.go gained TestEnsureDeployment_apiServerDefaults.
